concepts.name: Machine Learning Security
concepts.description: Defending machine learning systems from adversarial behavior targeting model parameters, pipelines, and infrastructure.

concepts.name: Machine Learning Privacy
concepts.description: Limiting the intentional and unintentional exposure of sensitive information within training data and model outputs.

concepts.name: Data Poisoning
concepts.description: Subtly manipulating training data to introduce a silent backdoor that activates on an attacker's chosen trigger.

concepts.name: Exact Model Theft
concepts.description: Gaining direct access to and extracting a model's internal components, including serialized files and weights.

concepts.name: Approximate Model Theft
concepts.description: Training a surrogate model that mimics the original by systematically observing input-output behavior through an API.

concepts.name: Model Inversion Attack
concepts.description: Querying a model through an interface to reverse-engineer and infer private details about the original training data.

concepts.name: Membership Inference Attack
concepts.description: Determining whether a specific individual's data record was included in a model's training dataset.

concepts.name: Speculative Execution Flaws
concepts.description: Hardware design vulnerabilities that exploit processor performance optimizations to leak sensitive data through microarchitectural side channels.

concepts.name: Physical Tampering
concepts.description: Direct, unauthorized manipulation of physical computing hardware to undermine the integrity of machine learning systems.

concepts.name: Hardware Trojans
concepts.description: Malicious, dormant circuits embedded during chip fabrication that trigger under specific conditions to disrupt computation or leak outputs.

concepts.name: Fault Injection Attack
concepts.description: Applying precisely timed physical disturbances, like voltage drops or lasers, to hardware to induce computational errors during inference.

concepts.name: Side-Channel Attack
concepts.description: Exploiting information inadvertently revealed through physical system implementation, such as power consumption or electromagnetic emissions.

concepts.name: Power Analysis Attack
concepts.description: A specific side-channel attack that measures power consumption patterns to infer internal system states or recover secret data.

concepts.name: Leaky Interfaces
concepts.description: Exposed communication or debug channels that unintentionally reveal sensitive information or accept unverified inputs.

concepts.name: Counterfeit Hardware
concepts.description: Unauthorized component reproductions that introduce reliability risks and potential hidden backdoors into machine learning supply chains.

concepts.name: Offensive Machine Learning
concepts.description: The weaponization of machine learning models to automate, accelerate, or enhance cyberattack operations against other systems.

concepts.name: Layered Defense
concepts.description: A security architecture employing multiple independent defensive mechanisms across data, model, runtime, and hardware layers.

concepts.name: Federated Learning
concepts.description: A structural privacy protection approach that keeps raw data localized on client devices while only transmitting model updates.

concepts.name: Gradient Inversion Attack
concepts.description: An attack that reconstructs raw original training data directly from the gradient updates transmitted by client devices.

concepts.name: Secure Aggregation
concepts.description: A cryptographic protocol ensuring a central server only computes the sum of gradient updates to prevent individual gradient inversion.

concepts.name: Homomorphic Encryption
concepts.description: A technique allowing machine learning inference or training to be computed directly on encrypted inputs without decrypting them.

concepts.name: Secure Multiparty Computation
concepts.description: Distributing computation across multiple parties such that no single entity learns the complete input or output data.

concepts.name: Differential Privacy
concepts.description: Adding calibrated noise to computations to provide a formal mathematical guarantee that no individual's data can be reverse-engineered.

concepts.name: Synthetic Data Generation
concepts.description: Training generative models to produce artificial data that preserves statistical properties of real data without retaining personally identifiable information.

concepts.name: Secure Model Design
concepts.description: Early-stage architectural choices, such as reducing capacity or adding abstention, to reduce a model's exposure to attacks.

concepts.name: Confidence Calibration
concepts.description: Designing models to accurately reflect uncertainty or abstain from predictions when faced with adversarial or out-of-distribution inputs.

concepts.name: Model Watermarking
concepts.description: Embedding verifiable, hidden ownership signatures directly into a model's parameters or output behavior to protect intellectual property.

concepts.name: Secure Model Deployment
concepts.description: Applying packaging, encryption, and access controls to protect a trained model during inference operations.

concepts.name: Runtime Input Validation
concepts.description: Ensuring incoming data conforms to expected formats, statistical properties, or semantic constraints before reaching the model.

concepts.name: Runtime Output Monitoring
concepts.description: Detecting adversarial behavior or distribution shifts by analyzing a model's prediction confidence and response patterns in real time.
